Arc Flash Study

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

CareLabs provides priced electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services to businesses of all sizes. To ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and comprehensive, certified professionals will analyze arc flash hazards, employee training, documentation, and PPE (Personal Protection Equipment) requirements. CareLabs can instantly identify arc flash threats and reduce the impact of harmonic distortions to safeguard the equipment. ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is utilized to investigate and evaluate Arc flashes. The amount of light and heat generated by an arc fault, also known as an arc flash. Something occurs when current flows across an air gap between conductors. The arc flash ionizes the atmosphere and produces a substantial amount of heat and light (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). As part of an arc flash analysis, the electrical installations of a facility are studied and assessed to determine the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash studies enhance workplace safety by defining criteria for qualified electricians to follow when operating equipment and circuits. Included as well are a short circuit and coordination analysis, new electrical one-line schematics, and equipment placements. 

Objectives of Arc Flash Research:

  • Determine how much damage was inflicted by the incident energy discharged by an arc flash fault.
  • modifying the settings of protective equipment to minimize exposure to incident energy
  • Describe the risks posed by arc flash safety regulations.
  • Respect the country’s specific laws and regulations.
  • Determine the type of PPE being used at work.

The Adverse Consequences of Arc Flash:

  • Arcs produce severe facial, ocular, and skin burns.
  • Inhaling gases and heated particles can severely burn the throat and lungs.
  • Flying debris and unprotected parts cause injury.

Arc flash studies are essential for reducing hazards and enhancing the safety of electrical employees and the entire organization. Arc flash investigations assist in identifying electrical hazard levels and implementing the appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of accidents and burns. All utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial companies employing important electrical components such as electrical disconnects, motor controllers, panel boards, and switches are required to undertake arc flash studies. 

Advantages of Arc Flash Study :

  • Increased human and mechanical safety
  • Lower medical and legal service expenses
  • Compliance with legal and safety regulations
  • Fewer rule violations and penalties.

Following are the techniques for doing an arc flash analysis:

  • Collection of data
  • Conducting a field inspection of a system’s design
  • Investigating arc flashes

Work Process in CareLabs in the Arc Flash Research:

CareLabs provides superior services and will fulfil any requirements made by the engineering firm, the construction team, or the local contractors.

The following steps constitute our approach of work:

  • Data collection
  • ETAP modeling of a power system
  • Short-circuit research
  • Coordination of overcurrent protection devices: a study
  • Investigating arc flashes
  • Investigating arc flashes
  • Maintaining records

Techniques for lowering the danger of an arc flash:

  • One-line diagrams that are large
  • Arc flash labeling installation
  • The finished document

CareLabs' Services Offer Several Advantages:

  • Improve the compliance and safety of electrical systems.
  • Integrated modules are linked with protective equipment and bus ratings for comprehensive short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device testing, and arc flash assessment.
  • Enhancing workplace security
  • Simple alternative design suggestion for the ideal shape
  • Using user-defined arc fault limits, safety margins were enhanced.
  • By producing arc flash labels automatically, our computer-aided analysis will save you time.
  • Reduce maintenance costs and productivity declines.

Crew members are stationed in various sections of the CareLabs to ensure their availability in the event of a regular or urgent occurrence. CareLabs has quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited firm, thanks to the positive response from its clients. All major cities, including Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, have access to our arc flash investigation and analysis services. Contact us immediately for a free estimate on our arc flash investigation and analysis. 

RELATED SERVICES

Arc Flash Study Arc flash analyses, also known as arc flash hazard analyses, are the process of looking at electrical systems to identify potential dangers related to arc flash accidents.
Read 01 05 Harmonic Study Harmonic voltage analysis is a type of mathematical method that is used to estimate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on the data that has been collected.


Read 02 05 Power Quality Study Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure that it satisfies the requirements necessary for the safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.
Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.

Home /Services /Harmonic Study and Analysis

Harmonic Study and
Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus

Terms of service?

Electrical Standards

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs provides a range of electrical installation services, including safety inspection, testing, calibration, and certification, in addition to comprehensive investigation and analysis of harmonics. Your electrical safety program will be reviewed by trained personnel to ensure that it is current, compliant, and comprehensive. In addition, these experts will evaluate harmonic threats, training materials, paperwork, and personal protection equipment (PPE). To ensure the equipment is running safely, CareLabs can detect harmonic distortions early on and take the necessary measures to mitigate their effect. Harmonic voltages and currents could degrade the performance of an electrical system. They may degrade the quality of the power and are inefficient heaters. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach for predicting resonances and degrees of harmonic distortion. 

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique that evaluates data to anticipate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Harmonic frequencies are introduced into the electrical system by nonlinear switching equipment, such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers, among others. When harmonic voltage and current levels are high, equipment failure is likely. Therefore, it is essential to study and analyze harmonics when appropriate. The current harmonic limit for 415-volt systems is 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Harmonic Distortion Effects:

  • It is responsible for current flow and high voltage.
  • Severe voltage distortion causes equipment failure and destruction
  • A rise in the quantity of internal energy in components results in higher fragility and a shorter life duration.
  • Errors in measurement cause branch circuit breakers to trip.
  • Systems of wiring and distribution exploding and burning
  • Generator failure
  • Fall in system power factor
  • The installation and utility costs are expensive.
  • Reduction in efficiency
  • Driving aimlessly and taking unnecessary breaks are not required.
  • Larger engines are employed

Harmonic issues are uncommon, but when they do occur, they tend to reduce the power system’s dependability, efficiency, and quality. Therefore, harmony must be explored and evaluated.

  • Protection against voltage and current harmonics
  • Frequency fluctuations restrict out-of-phase voltage and current.
  • Improve power factor analysis and maintain a value close to 1.
  • Regulates voltage peaks and troughs

With the assistance of harmonic investigation and analysis, it will be simpler to determine what may or may not be creating power quality issues. This will aid in preventing harmonics-related design issues and reduce the likelihood of undesirable outcomes. 

Harmonic Study and Analysis Workflow at CareLabs:

  • On the single-line schematic of an electrical circuit, call attention to the long medium-voltage cables, large capacitor banks, and nonlinear loads.
  • Identify the connection (PCC)
  • Determine which system buses in the facility are susceptible to harmonic distortions.
  • Collect harmonic information for all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain vital information on the maximum and minimum short circuit fault levels, as well as the harmonic levels.
  • ETAP is used to analyze the electrical network for harmonics.
  • Determine the overall and individual voltage and current wave distortion values of the bus and PCC.
  • Create the frequency spectrum of the harmonics.
  • If harmonic distortion levels are above the acceptable threshold, a remedy is proposed and implemented.
  • Rerun the analysis once the countermeasure has been implemented.

Advantages of CareLabs's offerings:

If background harmonics have previously been measured, higher ratings are applied in the second stage.

  • If the background is less than 25%, the size of the converter may change in stage 2 based on the fault level and kind of converter.
  • If the backdrop is between 25 and 75 percent, the converter ratings may be 50 percent lower.
  • A thorough harmonic analysis is performed on the top backdrop.

CareLabs employs crew members in a variety of locations to ensure that the workforce is accessible for both routine tasks and urgent situations. CareLabs immediately obtained ISO 9001:2008 accreditation and created a clientele with a positive reputation and high ratings. We conduct research and analysis in some of Hong Kong’s most prominent cities, including Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island Contact us immediately to organize your harmonic analysis and study in Hong Kong or to receive a quotation. 

RELATED SERVICES

Read 01 05 Harmonic Study Harmonic voltage analysis is a type of mathematical method that is used to estimate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on the data that has been collected.


Read 02 05 Power Quality Study Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure that it satisfies the requirements necessary for the safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.
Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.

Motor Start Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus

Terms of service?

Electrical Standards

RED

LRED

LRED

RED

LED

LRED

LRED

RED

ED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

Businesses in Hong Kong may utilize the Motor start research and analysis service provided by CareLabs. The primary objective of our motor start analysis service is to determine what peculiar occurrences occur when a large motor is started. There are more factors to consider before starting an electric motor in addition to ensuring that it is correctly connected to the line voltage. Unwanted tripping, high nominal currents, and fading or flickering lights are all indications that an electrical system needs maintenance. CareLabs will dispatch qualified staff to your location to collect both current and historical data on the motor installation. Using the gathered data and manufacturer’s information, calculations are then performed to estimate what will occur in the future and to devise solutions to mitigate any potential adverse consequences. 

Using static and dynamic modelling, one may investigate motor-starting techniques. We focus mostly on dynamic motor starting research that attempts to simulate motor starting conditions such as:

  • To simulate the load flow solution of an electric motor, its measured starting impedance is employed.
  • The voltage drop method is used to calculate the current in a motor when it is turned on for the first time, whereas the short-circuit method is used to compute the current under pre-fault short circuit conditions.
  • When an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be included into a typical transient stability technique to examine the consequences of the motor beginning.
  • In the lack of a comprehensive dynamic model of the electric motor, transient stability simulations may be employed.

Hong Kong Must Perform an Examination of Motor:

Modern industrial systems require motors, and the size of electric motors is expanding tremendously. If you switch on a large motor that is linked to the line, it might pose a threat to the electric motor, any local loads, and distant buses. For an electric motor to start, the terminal voltage must be at least 80% of the rated voltage or greater. Since the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is directly proportional to its load torque, voltage differential quickly influences the motor’s load torque characteristic.

If there is a significant voltage drop, the operating machines may endure greater stress than their “breakdown torque,” causing them to slow down or halt. Other types of loads, including lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment, are also affected by the voltage drop. In addition, high beginning current reduces terminal voltage, necessitating an increase in current from all systems that service a steady load. Broken relays for low voltages have also been discovered. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Required?

  • Prior to or at the time of purchasing a large engine.
  • If there is no generator and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
  • If the generator is the only source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15 percent of the generator’s KVA capacity, the generator should be replaced.
  • A motor start analysis may be required if many motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
  • Voltage drop issues are exacerbated when a motor with a lot of power is started, because smaller power supply often has less power. It may be feasible to gain a better understanding of the output of tiny generator-powered devices by examining how motors start.

An Analysis of the Benefits of Motor Start:

Analyses of how an engine begins Choose a motor or starting method with the optimal speed-torque characteristics for the task at hand. To prevent exceeding the maximum permitted torque of the apparatus, certain loads must be increased while being continuously monitored. A motor start analysis may always be used to examine the motor shaft. Consequently, it aids in determining how much torque the machinery can withstand. 

It is vital to do research on motors, beginning to determine how starting affects the voltage of the machine and if an electric motor can start and accelerate rapidly. CareLabs provides motor start tests conducted by trained experts to assist you evaluate the performance of your electric motor. In addition to Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, we offer motor start analysis services in all major cities. Get in contact with us as soon as possible to organize your motor starting study or to acquire a price.

Home /Services /Motor Start Analysis
Motor Start Analysis
Contact Us
LETS DISCOVER MORE

Businesses in Hong Kong may utilize the Motor start research and analysis service provided by CareLabs. The primary objective of our motor start analysis service is to determine what peculiar occurrences occur when a large motor is started. There are more factors to consider before starting an electric motor in addition to ensuring that it is correctly connected to the line voltage. Unwanted tripping, high nominal currents, and fading or flickering lights are all indications that an electrical system needs maintenance. CareLabs will dispatch qualified staff to your location to collect both current and historical data on the motor installation. Using the gathered data and manufacturer’s information, calculations are then performed to estimate what will occur in the future and to devise solutions to mitigate any potential adverse consequences. 

Using static and dynamic modelling, one may investigate motor-starting techniques. We focus mostly on dynamic motor starting research that attempts to simulate motor starting conditions such as:

To simulate the load flow solution of an electric motor, its measured starting impedance is employed.
The voltage drop method is used to calculate the current in a motor when it is turned on for the first time, whereas the short-circuit method is used to compute the current under pre-fault short circuit conditions.
When an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be included into a typical transient stability technique to examine the consequences of the motor beginning.
In the lack of a comprehensive dynamic model of the electric motor, transient stability simulations may be employed.
Hong Kong Must Perform an Examination of Motor:

Modern industrial systems require motors, and the size of electric motors is expanding tremendously. If you switch on a large motor that is linked to the line, it might pose a threat to the electric motor, any local loads, and distant buses. For an electric motor to start, the terminal voltage must be at least 80% of the rated voltage or greater. Since the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is directly proportional to its load torque, voltage differential quickly influences the motor’s load torque characteristic.

If there is a significant voltage drop, the operating machines may endure greater stress than their “breakdown torque,” causing them to slow down or halt. Other types of loads, including lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment, are also affected by the voltage drop. In addition, high beginning current reduces terminal voltage, necessitating an increase in current from all systems that service a steady load. Broken relays for low voltages have also been discovered. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Required?
Prior to or at the time of purchasing a large engine.
If there is no generator and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
If the generator is the only source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15 percent of the generator’s KVA capacity, the generator should be replaced.
A motor start analysis may be required if many motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
Voltage drop issues are exacerbated when a motor with a lot of power is started, because smaller power supply often has less power. It may be feasible to gain a better understanding of the output of tiny generator-powered devices by examining how motors start.
An Analysis of the Benefits of Motor Start:

Analyses of how an engine begins Choose a motor or starting method with the optimal speed-torque characteristics for the task at hand. To prevent exceeding the maximum permitted torque of the apparatus, certain loads must be increased while being continuously monitored. A motor start analysis may always be used to examine the motor shaft. Consequently, it aids in determining how much torque the machinery can withstand. 

It is vital to do research on motors, beginning to determine how starting affects the voltage of the machine and if an electric motor can start and accelerate rapidly. CareLabs provides motor start tests conducted by trained experts to assist you evaluate the performance of your electric motor. In addition to Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, we offer motor start analysis services in all major cities. Get in contact with us as soon as possible to organize your motor starting study or to acquire a price.

Home /Services /Power Quality Analysis

Power Quality
Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus

Terms of service?

Electrical Standards

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs examines the quality of the power for companies around Honk Kong. Our objective is to prevent electrical equipment, appliances, and installations from malfunctioning due to fluctuations in the power supply. Numerous organizations and a rising number of individual customers utilize data gathering, monitoring, and measurement equipment that is particularly sensitive to fluctuations in the power supply. Most of the electrical equipment is designed to accommodate specific supply changes, but few are designed to accommodate the wide range of voltage swings typical with distributed power. CareLabs provides the expertise and experience required to assist you with equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning difficulties.

The Purpose of Power Quality Analysis Is To:

  • Locating the load’s and the electrical system’s issues’ origin.
  • Utilizing the workplace to enhance simulation methodologies for discovering answers to power quality issues.
  • Assessing the performance and efficiency of heavy equipment
  • Enhance the machine’s overall performance and power factor.
  • Try not to exert excessive energy.
  • To prevent things from breaking down and halting.
  • Removing power-degrading problems such as transients, harmonics, and spikes.
  • Prevent voltage and frequency spikes as well as PLC stalling.
  • To prevent problems such as capacitor explosions, transformer overheating, and nuisance tripping.
  • Significantly reduces your power expenditure.
  • Extend the life of the electrical network and its components.
  • Determine if the electrical equipment can resist alterations.

Hong Kong Should Evaluate Its Energy Supply:

All electrical networks endure power outages, which occur more often when storms, accidents, or other natural catastrophes destroy power lines. Variations in power supply are quite natural. There are three sorts of alterations, interruptions, and distortions that affect the ability of an electrical network to transmit power:

1. A frequency deviation occurs when a frequency strays from its native frequency range.

2.Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage’s amplitude exceeds its normal range, but its shape stays unchanged.

2.1 Voltage spikes and dips, both lasting up to a minute, are examples of short-term fluctuations.

2.2 Under voltages and excess voltages are examples of persistent fluctuations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This problem causes the equipment to overheat when the load is not distributed evenly throughout the phases.

2.4 Difference in voltage between neutral and earth

3. Disturbances caused by distortions that occur when the voltage contour is not even or smooth.

3.1 Due to harmonics and inter harmonics, there are resonance issues, overheating, power loss, and power loss in electronic devices.

3.2 Transients with durations of less than one-tenth of a second have the potential to damage switch contact surfaces, reduce system performance, and accelerate the failure of motor drives and control.

What Does Power Quality Analysis Comprise?

To identify various power quality concerns, several methods must be employed. Among these tactics are the following:

  • Utilizing computer algorithms and reduction approaches, harmonic analysis identifies and anticipates probable harmonic difficulties.
  • This examination will uncover surges and transients.
  • Study of Voltage Dips and Climbs This analysis would monitor the voltage’s value, trajectory, and short-term dips and climbs.
  • This study will identify the optimal reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
  • This analysis would examine and coordinate captive power to satisfy demand while minimizing fuel and energy surcharges.
  • This research determines the magnitudes of power flows, power factors, voltage levels, and losses in system branches depending on the operating circumstances.

To enhance the dependability and quality of its clients’ electrical systems, CareLabs provides the finest service possible. In addition to Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, we provide power quality diagnostic services in every other major city. Contact us immediately to book a power quality assessment or to obtain a price. 

RELATED SERVICES

Arc Flash Study Arc flash analyses, also known as arc flash hazard analyses, are the process of looking at electrical systems to identify potential dangers related to arc flash accidents.
Read 01 05 Harmonic Study Harmonic voltage analysis is a type of mathematical method that is used to estimate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on the data that has been collected.


Read 02 05 Power Quality Study Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure that it satisfies the requirements necessary for the safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.
Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.
Read 05 05

Power System Study
and Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus

Terms of service?

Electrical Standards

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs provides electrical installation services including power quality analysis studies, electrical safety inspections, simulations of electrical system design, as well as research and analysis of power systems. Our team of electrical professionals conducts load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies utilizing ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the industry’s premier power system transient analysis tool. It enables us to collaborate directly with our clients to create and implement the most effective power compensation and solutions. 

Why Would an Examination of the Electricity System Be Conducted?

Every industrial or commercial structure must have a continuous and dependable power source. Even a little power loss or failure can cause significant difficulties, such as extended downtime, damage to vital equipment, and repair costs. On a regular basis, your building’s power system might undergo an examination to ensure that it is dependable, safe, and efficient under normal and abnormal conditions. A complete study is necessary at each stage of the design, installation, and operation of a power system. 

Using the ETAP software tool, we will examine and analyze the power system to gain an understanding of its regular and emergency operation. It is a compendium of engineering studies that examines the transient, dynamic, and static responses of an electric power system. To evaluate the status of your institution’s electrical system, many studies may be conducted. It might be included in a report on the power system’s study. Here are some examples of this type:

  • Interrupter for the circuit
  • Developing a security plan
  • Load Movement
  • Varying evaluations across time
  • Arc Flash: Investigating It
  • What is the power’s efficiency?
  • Harmonics evaluation
  • launching the automobile
  • Geological investigation

It may be used to define standards for the electrical safety program as well as other aspects of plant operation. These studies might establish the dependability and security of operations. 

An Analysis of Load Circulation:

By conducting load flow studies to discover the voltages and currents in various network segments, you may select the appropriate rated equipment. Using ETAP’s built-in load flow function, you can determine a power system’s voltages and currents, as well as the amount of power being consumed and its power factor. 

Studies of Short Circuits:

It is vital to conduct short-circuit studies to comprehend how a problem impacts the functioning of a power system. Manually investigating short circuits takes a significant amount of time; software like ETAP that can perform the task accurately is far faster. ETAP is utilized to simulate system protection devices to assess the influence of 3-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures on distribution systems. 

Temporal Analysis:

Studies of transients identify the magnitude and nature of overvoltage and current surges caused by lightning strikes and switching operations. You may select how much insulation and surge protection you need based on the results. 

Protection Coordination Research:

H2: Protection Coordination Research:

This research aims to ensure that the security components of your system are functioning properly. It verifies that they are the correct size and fit the object they are protecting.

Typically, our power system analysis research methodology comprises the three critical steps:

  • How to Acquire Data
  • Discussions with clients and data analysis
  • Getting equipment details
  • A basic one-line diagram
  • ETAP modelling of power system models
  • Check the current load and the short circuit.
  • Consider coordinating the security.
  • An analysis of arc flashes
  • A summary of the whole research report
  • Risk appraisal and management
  • Project administration

The Following Are the Components of Carelabs' Power System Analysis:

  • Chances to reduce energy expenses and use.
  • Reports that bring up probable flaws.
  • Suggestions for cures
  • A description of where and how energy is utilized.
  • Confirmation of protective equipment ratings
  • Assist in protecting against danger and damage.
  • Harmonic and transient effects

In our power system analysis studies, we examine the client’s electrical system and give recommendations on how to improve its performance and ensure that power is always available. In addition to Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, we examine and evaluate the electrical infrastructure of other major cities in Hong Kong. Contact us immediately to schedule a power system evaluation and analysis or to obtain a price.
